decontamination_notes: Real bug fix from BurntSushi/jiff PR #527 (fixes issue #526), merged 2026-02-28, after model training cutoffs. TryFrom<SignedDuration> for std::time::Duration panicked instead of returning Err when the signed duration had a zero whole-seconds component and a negative subsecond component (e.g. SignedDuration::new(0, -1)): the seconds were converted fallibly but the nanoseconds used u32::try_from(sd.subsec_nanos()).unwrap(). The fix maps the nanos conversion failure to the same SpecialBoundsError (renamed UnsignedDurationSeconds -> SignedToUnsignedDuration with a corrected Display message). Repo sliced at the PR base commit (c3d960e). To build in the network-isolated sandbox with zero external dependencies, jiff is trimmed to a standalone single crate (workspace sub-crates, optional deps, dev-deps and existing integration tests removed) that compiles fully offline with --no-default-features --features std; no vendoring is required. LOC is the real jiff source. Unlicense/MIT LICENSE preserved. Coverage floor: 3 fail_to_pass tests, each a distinct zero-seconds/negative-subsecond input that panics at base and returns Err after the fix, plus 3 pass_to_pass regression guards (non-negative roundtrip, negative-seconds-already-errors, forward Duration->SignedDuration roundtrip) that hold at base and do not reference the fixed path.
